| First game            = {{fb|SLV}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|NCA|1908}}<br>([[San Salvador]], El Salvador; 1 May 1929)

| 3rdRegional cup apps  = 6
| 3rdRegional cup first = [[1941 CCCF Championship|1941]]
| 3rdRegional cup best  = Third place ([[1951 CCCF Championship|1951]])
| 4thRegional name      = [[Copa Centroamericana]]
| 4thRegional cup apps  = 14
| 4thRegional cup first = [[1991 UNCAF Nations Cup|1991]]
| 4thRegional cup best  = Fifth place ([[2005 UNCAF Nations Cup|2005]], [[2009 UNCAF Nations Cup|2009]], [[2017 Copa Centroamericana|2017]])

{{MedalCompetition|[[CCCF Championship]]}}
{{MedalBronze|[[1951 CCCF Championship|1951 Panama]]|Team}}
|pattern_so3=_nca24t|pattern_sh3=_nca24t|pattern_b3=_nca24t|pattern_la3=_nca24t|pattern_ra3=_nca24t|socks3=000000|shorts3=000000}}

The '''Nicaragua national football team''' ({{langx|es|Selección de fútbol de Nicaragua}}) represents [[Nicaragua]] in men's international [[Association football|football]], which is governed by the [[Nicaraguan Football Federation|Federación Nicaragüense de Fútbol]] ({{langx|en|Nicaraguan Football Federation}}) founded in 1931. It has been an affiliate member of [[FIFA]] since 1950 and a founding affiliate member of [[CONCACAF]] since 1961. Regionally, it is an affiliate member of [[Central American Football Union|UNCAF]] in the Central American Zone.

Nicaragua has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup]], but has participated five times in CONCACAF's premier continental competition, finishing sixth place in the [[1967 CONCACAF Championship]]. It has participated three times in League B and once in League A of the [[CONCACAF Nations League]].

Regionally, the team finished in third place in the [[CCCF Championship]] in [[1957 CCCF Championship|1957]], and also finished in fifth place three times in the [[Copa Centroamericana]].<ref>{{cite web|publisher=[[CONCACAF]]|title=Nicaragua shocks Guatemala 2–0 to claim last berth in CONCACAF Gold Cup|url=http://www.concacaf.com/view_article.aspx?id=4610|date=2009-01-29|access-date=2009-01-29|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20090202060729/http://concacaf.com/view_article.aspx?id=4610|archive-date=2009-02-02}}</ref>

Nicaragua's first appearance in international competition was in the [[1941 CCCF Championship]]. Their first appearance in World Cup qualifiers was in the [[1994 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1994 CONCACAF qualification]].

==History==
Nicaragua traveled to [[El Salvador]] for their first ever international, losing 9–0 to the hosts on 1 May 1929. They did not play another match for over twelve years, until the qualifying group for the [[1941 CCCF Championship]] in [[Costa Rica]]. They lost all 4 games: 7–2 to the hosts [[Costa Rica national football team|Costa Rica]] on 10 May, 8–0 to El Salvador on 13 May, 9–1 to the [[Netherlands Antilles national football team|Netherlands Antilles]] on 15 May and 5–2 to [[Panama national football team|Panama]] on 18 May. Therefore, Nicaragua finished bottom of the group and did not make the final group.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesn/nica-intres.html|title=Nicaragua - List of International Matches|work=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=4 January 2017|archive-date=24 February 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230224193802/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesn/nica-intres.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

===Recent times===
After qualifying for the [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019 Gold Cup]] on the back of finishing ninth in [[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League qualifying|Nations League qualifying]], they would go on to finish bottom of the group with losses to Costa Rica, [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]] and [[Bermuda national football team|Bermuda]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.goldcup.org/en/article/bermuda-downs-nicaragua-to-earn-first-ever-gold-cup-victory|title=Bermuda downs Nicaragua to earn first ever Gold Cup victory|location=New Jersey|date=24 June 2019|access-date=9 August 2019|publisher=Gold Cup|archive-date=6 March 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210306173450/https://www.goldcup.org/en/article/bermuda-downs-nicaragua-to-earn-first-ever-gold-cup-victory|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Nicaragua qualified directly for the [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023 Gold Cup]] and was promoted to [[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League A|Nations League A]] as group winners in [[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League B|Nations League B]] but was disqualified on 12 June 2023 for fielding an ineligible player. As a result, they were replaced in the Gold Cup and League A by [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]], who was the best second-place team overall in League B and must stay in [[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League B|Nations League B]].<ref name="Nicaragua">{{cite press release |date=12 June 2023 |title=Concacaf Disciplinary Committee decision regarding Nicaragua and the Eligibility of Players |url=https://www.concacaf.com/news/concacaf-disciplinary-committee-decision-regarding-nicaragua-and-the-eligibility-of-players/ |publisher=CONCACAF |accessdate=12 June 2023}}</ref>

===Coaching history===
{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{Flagicon|Nicaragua|1908}} [[Octavio Edmundo Salas Urbina]] (1941–1943)
*{{Flagicon|Hungary|1920}} [[Eduardo Kosovic]] (1943)
*{{Flagicon|Costa Rica}} José Antonio ''Pipa'' Cordero (1947)
*{{Flagicon|Costa Rica}} [[Santiago Bonilla]] (1952–1953)
*{{Flagicon|Hungary}} [[Francisc Mészáros|Ferenc Mészáros]] (1961)
*{{Flagicon|Nicaragua|1908}} [[Carlos Ruiz (soccer)|Carlos Ruiz]] (1961–1962)
*{{Flagicon|Argentina}} [[Santiago Berrini]] (1965–1967)
*{{Flagicon|Nicaragua|1908}} [[Livio Bendaña Espinoza]] (1967–1969)
*{{Flagicon|Argentina}} [[Omar Muraco]] (1969–1970)
*{{Flagicon|Nicaragua}} [[Salvador Dubois Leiva]] (1977, 1991–1992)
*{{Flagicon|Argentina}} [[Orlando Casares]] (1980–1981)
*{{flagicon|Czechoslovakia}} [[Ján Fülöp]] (1984–1986)
*{{Flagicon|Nicaragua}} [[Mauricio Cruz Jiron]] (1993–2001, 2008)
*{{Flagicon|Nicaragua}} [[Florencio Levia]] (2001–2002)
*{{Flagicon|Italy|2003}} [[Maurizio Battistini (football manager)|Maurizio Battistini]] (2003–2004)
*{{Flagicon|Argentina}} [[Marcelo Javier Zuleta]] (2005)
*{{flagicon|Argentina}} [[Carlos Alberto de Toro|Carlos de Toro]] (2007–2008)
*{{Flagicon|Nicaragua}} [[Ramón Otoniel Olivas]] (2008–2009, 2026)
*{{Flagicon|Spain}} [[Enrique Llena]] (2010–2014)
*{{Flagicon|Colombia}} [[Javier Londoño]] (2014)
*{{Flagicon|Costa Rica}} [[Henry Duarte]] (2014–2020)
*{{Flagicon|Argentina}} [[Juan Vita]] (2020–2022)
*{{Flagicon|Chile}} [[Marco Antonio Figueroa]] (2022–2025)
*{{Flag icon|Argentina}} [[Juan Cruz Real]] (2026-present)
*{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up to the squad for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly]] match against [[Russia national football team|Russia]] on 27 March 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/laazulyblanco/posts/pfbid0gN2kiv2BX45DfWCMmLeY8AFP3rxnT5YFjEHpwt6naT3fYNBvVfE2UZFMS21Rxun5l|title=Estos son los convocados del profe Otoniel Olivas de la Selección Mayor Masculina para disputar el amistoso internacional ante Rusia|publisher=[[Nicaraguan Football Federation]]|date=22 March 2026|language=es|via=[[Facebook]]}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 14 October 2024, after the match against [[French Guiana national football team|French Guiana]].''

{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[José Martínez (footballer, born 2005)|José Martínez]]|age={{Birth date and age|2005|2|13|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Managua FC|Managua]]|clubnat=NCA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=FW|name=[[Byron Bonilla]]|age={{Birth date and age|1993|8|30|df=y}}|caps=35|goals=6|club=[[Real Estelí FC|Real Estelí]]|clubnat=NCA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Juan Barrera]]|other=[[Captain (association football)|captain]]|age={{Birth date and age|1989|5|2|df=y}}|caps=93|goals=26|club=[[Real Estelí FC|Real Estelí]]|clubnat=NCA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=FW|name=[[Jorge García (footballer, born 1998)|Jorge García]]|age={{Birth date and age|1998|8|27|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Matagalpa FC|Matagalpa]]|clubnat=NCA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=FW|name=[[Edgar Castillo (footballer)|Edgar Castillo]]|age={{Birth date and age|2002|5|21|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Club Sport Sebaco|Sébaco]]|clubnat=NCA}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for the team in the last twelve months.
<!--- PLEASE NOTE PLAYERS STAY 12 MONTHS AND NOT LESS THAN THAT -->

{{nat fs r player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Brandon Mayorga]]|age={{Birth date and age|1998|1|6|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[UNAN Managua]]|clubnat=NCA|latest=v. {{fb|HAI}}, 18 November 2025}}

{{nat fs r player|no=7|pos=FW|name=[[Brayan López (footballer, born 1990)|Brayan López]]|age={{Birth date and age|1990|6|3|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Puntarenas F.C.|Puntarenas]]|clubnat=CRC|latest=v. {{fb|HAI}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Jaime Moreno (footballer, born 1995)|Jaime Moreno]]|age={{Birth date and age|1995|3|30|df=y}}|caps=42|goals=13|club=[[Barito Putera]]|clubnat=IDN|latest=v. {{fb|HAI}}, 18 November 2025}} 
{{nat fs r player|no=13|pos=FW|name=[[Ariel Arauz]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|5|30|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[C.S. Herediano|Herediano]]|clubnat=CRC|latest=v. {{fb|HAI}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=20|pos=FW|name=[[Widman Talavera]]|age={{Birth date and age|2003|1|12|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=3|club=[[Real Estelí FC|Real Estelí]]|clubnat=NCA|latest=v. {{fb|HAI}}, 18 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br>
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br>
<sup>RET</sup> Player retired from the national team.<br>
<sup>SUS</sup> Player is serving suspension.<br>
<sup>WD</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.<br>
<sup>INE</sup> Ineligible player.<br>
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{updated|9 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Passo Alpuin |first1=Luis Fernando |title=Nicaragua – Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/nica-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=5 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230405223800/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/nica-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Nicaragua.''

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
The Nicaragua national football team has never qualified for the World Cup. Their latest attempt was for the [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022 World Cup]], when the side failed to advance from the [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF first round|first round]].

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!width=1% rowspan=28|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record

!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
